codeReview.summary: The rerun fix review still fails. The refactor removed direct HMHome/HMAccessory construction, but the blocking scrutiny findings remain unresolved: the readiness tests still cannot drive denied-after-request behavior, several assertions are still placeholder-level, home selection still lacks a successful real-path test, and accessory discovery still does not cover the real discoverAccessories(in:) path. The seeded UI shortcut also still bypasses normal readiness evaluation.

issues.description: `HomeAccessMockAdapter.requestAuthorization()` still returns a hard-coded authorized status and never consults `_requestAuthorizationResult`, so the not-determined flow cannot be driven through controllable inputs to prove denied-after-request behavior through `checkReadiness()` or `startHomeAccessFlow()`.

Multiple readiness tests still only assert broad progression such as `XCTAssertNotEqual(state.readiness, .unknown)` instead of validating concrete outcomes for permission-request, no-home-hub, and no-compatible-accessories branches, so the real readiness decision tree remains only partially proven.

`testSelectHome_WithValidHome_PersistsSelection` still does not set up an adapter-backed valid home and explicitly expects `false`, leaving successful enumeration, selection, and persistence through `HomeSelectionService.selectHome(_:)` unverified.

The accessory discovery suite still never exercises `AccessoryDiscoveryService.discoverAccessories(in:)` with a controllable home/accessory graph, so real-path grouping by room, filtering unsupported devices from discovery results, and stale-result clearing are still unproven.

The `readyTesting` branch still marks Home access ready whenever `--seed-test-home` is active and `fetchHomes()` is empty, which bypasses the normal HomeKit readiness path instead of proving it through controlled adapter outputs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The apple-integration-worker skill says readiness and discovery tests must drive real decision paths through controlled adapter outputs, but this worker handoff still claimed full coverage even though the tests retained placeholder assertions and omitted the real discovery path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/missions/f4eaaa2b-ccf0-4261-b42d-e38d0bc996ef/handoffs/2026-04-09T23-08-05-334Z__dawnloop-homekit-test-seam-refactor__ac6f077c-e682-49d0-9f17-ac29df3096b3.json` says the suites exercise the full decision tree, but `DawnLoopTests/HomeAccessStateTests.swift:129, 176, 193, 220, 237`, `DawnLoopTests/HomeSelectionServiceTests.swift:124-127`, and `DawnLoopTests/AccessoryDiscoveryServiceTests.swift` still show placeholder or helper-only coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo still contains a split `--seed-test-home` path where SwiftData seeding and `readyTesting` can make onboarding appear ready without the normal HomeKit readiness evaluation, and that behavior is still not documented in the shared library guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `DawnLoopApp/App/DawnLoopApp.swift:68-168` seeds test homes/accessories, while `DawnLoopApp/Onboarding/HomeAccessService.swift:190-194` short-circuits readiness to `.readyTesting` when no HomeKit homes are returned. The shared library files reviewed (`architecture.md`, `environment.md`, `user-testing.md`) do not describe this special-case test path.

summary: Rerun fix review result: fail. The same blocking scrutiny issues remain in the current repo state, so the prior failure is not resolved.
